steps.title: Count values around the median
steps.content: There are $29$ different numbers, so the median is the $15$th value. Since the median is $34$, there are $14$ values below $34$, one value equal to $34$, and $14$ values above $34$.

steps.title: Check sum and mean
steps.content:
The values above the median each increase by $6$, and the values below the median each decrease by $6$.
$$
14(6)-14(6)=0
$$
The sum does not change, so the mean does not change.

steps.title: Check spread from the center
steps.content: The median value stays at $34$, but every value below it moves lower and every value above it moves higher. The data become more spread out, so the standard deviation changes. The answer is D.
